title: Broad Phase, Circle, Rect, Mask, Seam, and Swept Collision Detection · Language Clinic: Circle Distance
summary: The game tests every pair, uses transformed Rect overlap as exact geometry, constructs masks every frame, misses seam pairs, and lets fast bullets tunnel through small rocks. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the circle distance increment in layered collision detection pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
Use groups, spatial partitioning, sweep, or bounded pair sets to reduce candidates without dropping possible overlaps.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-circle-distance — Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_collision_detection_3(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Circle Distance evidence · Theory Circle Distance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-circle-distance-78glup" that uses case 7532 to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Circle Distance into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Circle Distance evidence · Theory Circle Distance, keep Circle Distance connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-circle-distance-78glup
# Competency: asteroids-circle-distance.
# Case 7532: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_circle_distance_78glup(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_78glup = "78glup-pulg87-7532"
    assert evidence_variant_78glup
    evidence_axes_78glup = (
        "player78glup",
        "seed7532",
        "inputpulg87",
        "frame78glup7532",
        "state753278glup",
        "playtestpulg877532",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_78glup)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Circle Distance model · Theory Circle Distance
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Circle Distance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4931 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
